title: 2015-16 Departmental Performance Report - Canada.ca
content:
## 2015-16 Departmental Performance Report

## Appendix E – Other Items of Interest

### Public Service Commission and Assessment of recourse reports
The Canada Revenue Agency Act requires the CRA to include in its annual reporting a copy of any report produced under section 59 or a summary statement of any assessment produced under subsection 56(1). The report and assessment are produced on an as needed basis.
During 2015-2016, the Public Service Commission did not prepare, or have prepared on its behalf, a report to the CRA pursuant to section 59 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act on the consistency of the Agency’s staffing program with the principles set out in the summary of its corporate business plan. For the same period, the CRA did not prepare pursuant to subsection 56(1) of the Canada Revenue Agency Act an assessment of the recourse the Agency provides or administers in its management of human resources.

### Benefit programs and benefit-related services delivered by the Canada Revenue Agency

#### Six federal benefit programs
Canada child tax benefit
Disability tax credit
Goods and services tax/harmonized sales tax credit
Children’s special allowances
Universal child care benefit – on behalf of Employment and Social Development Canada
Working income tax benefit advance payments
British Columbia – BC family bonus
Saskatchewan low-income tax credit
Alberta family employment tax credit
Newfoundland and Labrador – mother baby nutrition supplement
New Brunswick child tax benefit
Ontario child benefit
New Brunswick working income supplement
British Columbia low-income climate action tax credit
Newfoundland and Labrador harmonized sales tax credit
Ontario senior homeowners’ property tax grant
British Columbia – BC earned income benefit
Ontario sales tax credit
Saskatchewan child benefit
British Columbia harmonized sales tax credit
Northwest Territories child benefit
Nova Scotia affordable living tax credit
Northwest Territories – territorial worker’s supplement
Ontario energy and property tax credit
Nova Scotia child benefit
Northern Ontario energy credit
Ontario trillium benefit
Nunavut child benefit
New Brunswick school supplement
Nunavut – territorial worker’s supplement
Prince Edward Island sales tax and credit
Newfoundland and Labrador child benefit
British Columbia early childhood tax benefit
Newfoundland and Labrador seniors’ benefit
Relief for heating expenses (federal)
Energy cost benefit (federal)
British Columbia – BC energy rebate
Alberta 2005 resource rebate
Alberta energy tax refund
Ontario home electricity relief
Ontario taxpayer dividend
British Columbia climate action dividend
Nova Scotia taxpayer refund
Ontario sales tax transition benefit
Sixty-five income verification data exchanges using File Transfer Protocol ( FTP ) with provinces and territories to support programs
Ten national child benefit supplement data exchanges with provinces and territories to facilitate the calculation of social assistance
Seven data transfers with provinces to support top-ups for CRA administered child benefit programs
Two data transfers of income and child information to support administration of the Ontario child care supplement and Quebec family allowance programs
Eight data exchanges and transfers to support the Nova Scotia pharmacare program, the British Columbia low-income climate action tax credit/BC harmonized sales tax credit, Ontario senior homeowners property tax grant, Ontario sales tax credit, Ontario energy and property tax credit/Northern Ontario energy credit, Ontario trillium benefit, Prince Edward Island sales tax credit and to assist the ministère du Revenu du Québec in administering the Quebec Taxation Act
Ten data exchanges and data transfers to support federal administration of the employment insurance family supplement, guaranteed income supplement, Canada learning bond, Canada education savings grant (basic grant and additional grant), Canada disability savings bond, Canada disability savings grant, Old Age Security recovery tax, universal child care benefit and employment insurance long tenured workers program
